name: Deep-Fried Sesame Seed Ball - Jien Duy
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the glutinous rice powder in a mixing bowl.
2. Bring the boiling water to a boil and add the Chinese brown sugar; stir until completely dissolved. Pour the sugar solution into the rice powder; stir until well mixed.
3. Gather the dough into a ball and knead until smooth.
4. Cut the dough in half and roll each half into two 10-inch long rolls.
5. Cut each roll into 10 pieces. Take one piece and roll it into a ball.
6. Flatten the ball slightly and place a 1/2-inch round piece of sweet red bean paste into the center of the dough.
7. Fold over the sides to enclose the filling and roll into a ball. Repeat until all the balls are made.
8. Wet your hands with water and roll one ball in your hands to moisten the surface slightly.
9. Dip the ball into a dish of sesame seeds until the entire surface is covered.
10. Press lightly to help the seeds adhere.
11. Add enough oil to a wok to a depth of 2 1/2 inches. Heat to 350°F.
12. Deep fry the balls for 8 to 10 minutes, or until they expand in the oil and triple in size, float to the top of the oil, and turn golden brown.
13. Remove from the oil and drain on paper towels. Makes about 20 balls.
